title: S&P 500, Nasdaq end higher, notch weekly gains after earnings-heavy week
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq ended higher, marking their sixth consecutive weekly gains, driven by strong tech earnings, particularly from Apple. This streak is their
excerpt: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq ended higher, marking their sixth consecutive weekly gains, driven by strong tech earnings, particularly from Apple. This streak is their longest since October 2024.

content.introduction: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq ended higher, marking their sixth consecutive weekly gains, driven by strong tech earnings, particularly from Apple. This streak is their longest since October 2024.

## Apple Inc. | 280.14 USD | +3.24% | Intraday chart for Apple Inc. Intraday chart for Apple Inc. STORY: U.S. stocks ended mostly higher on Friday, with the Dow the only outlier, dipping about three-tenths of a percent, while the S&P 500 gained about three-tenths of a percent and the Nasdaq climbed nine-tenths of a percent. Among them, Apple climbed more than 3% on Friday, a day after the tech giant provided a solid sales forecast, touting strong demand for its flagship iPhone 17 and the MacBook Neo. Analysts now see overall first-quarter earnings growth of nearly 28% year-over-year, according to LSEG I/B/E/S Estimates. | May. 01 | S&P 500, Nasdaq end higher, notch weekly gains after earnings-heavy week | RE |. | May. 01 | S&P 500, Nasdaq advance, set for weekly gains after earnings-heavy week | RE |.
